steps.title: Find the sample proportion
steps.content:
The sample had $10$ supporters out of $25$ people. Use the sample as an estimate for the whole county board because the poll was random:
$$\frac{10}{25}=0.40$$

steps.title: Scale to the full board
steps.content: Apply the sample proportion to all $60$ board members to estimate the number of supporters in the full group: $$0.40\cdot60=24$$ The answer is C.
